items.title: [Virtual Event] Anatomy of a Data Breach: What to Do if it Happens to You
Firewalla adds AI analysis to Managed Security Portal
items.summary: Small IT teams and MSPs get fewer false alerts as Firewalla's latest portal update adds AI threat scoring and clearer device health views.

items.title: Rubrik & MEDITECH team up on hospital cyber resilience
items.summary: Hospitals using MEDITECH Expanse could restore records faster after ransomware, as Rubrik adds recovery tools across cloud and on-premises systems.

items.title: Browse news and alerts - page 14 - Scamwatch
items.summary: Browse news and alerts - page 14 Scamwatch

items.title: Geordie links Claude Enterprise to security controls
items.summary: Enterprise teams can now monitor chats, files and project logs in Claude, closing a security gap as AI tools take on more workplace tasks.

items.title: Coro signs Australian distribution deal with Leader
items.summary: Australian MSPs and resellers gain access to Coro's cybersecurity platform as the deal broadens channel options and simplifies security management.

items.title: CTERA launches InsightAI for unstructured data analysis
items.summary: It gives IT and security teams a way to spot risks, track usage and investigate incidents across sprawling unstructured data estates.

items.title: Darwinium updates mobile SDKs to spot scam activity
items.summary: Banks and payment firms could spot scams mid-session, as Darwinium's updated mobile SDKs track live calls, screen sharing and device evasion.

items.title: MIND first data security firm in Anthropic cyber scheme
items.summary: Broader Claude access should help MIND sharpen data discovery and loss prevention for customers, after it joined Anthropic's cyber scheme.

items.title: Black Kite says 58 CVEs pose critical supply threat
items.summary: Only a small fraction of disclosed flaws are likely to hit suppliers, leaving security teams to focus on the 58 highest-risk CVEs.

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
items.summary: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. Krebs

items.title: How Deepfakes Tore a High School Apart
items.summary: After five teen girls were targeted by AI-generated child sexual abuse material, Radnor Township High School in Pennsylvania has become a case study in how schools and police around the country grapple with how to response to deepfake crimes involving children.

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.summary: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.summary: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
items.summary: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: Processes & Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
items.summary: In spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents shows issues persist and visibility falls short.

items.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
items.summary: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

items.title: Township Leader Resigns in Tears Over OpenAI Data Center Death Threats
items.summary: "I can’t take it anymore. The threats," the treasurer said. The planned data center is part of OpenAI and Oracle's Stargate initiative.

items.title: Podcast: Elites Just Don't Get AI
items.summary: Commencement speeches, poop images to train AI, and cameras stuck to preschool teachers also to train AI.

items.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
items.summary: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.

items.title: Risky Business #838 -- GitHub investigates possible breach
items.summary: On this week’s show Patrick Gray, Adam Boileau and James Wilson discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. They cover: GitHub announced a possible breach CISA leaks important creds, keys in public repo Awful vulnerability in Bitlocker renders it useless without a PIN So. Many. Patches. Polish Government

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
items.summary: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

items.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
items.summary: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

items.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
items.summary: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

items.title: Browse news and alerts - Scamwatch
items.summary: Browse news and alerts Scamwatch

items.title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
items.summary: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
items.summary: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

items.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
items.summary: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

items.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
items.summary: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.

items.title: Researchers Wanted Preschool Teachers to Wear Cameras to Train AI
items.summary: “With your permission, your child’s lead teacher may wear a small teacher-worn camera that captures the teacher's approximate first-person perspective, and/or we may place a fixed video camera in the classroom,” a document given to parents and later shared with 404 Media reads.

items.title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
items.summary: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

items.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
items.summary: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failures, and cringeworthy moments that still have us shaking our heads.

items.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
items.summary: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

items.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
items.summary: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

items.title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
items.summary: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

items.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
items.summary: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

items.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
items.summary: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

items.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
items.summary: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

items.title: Welcoming the Bahamian Government to Have I Been Pwned
items.summary: Today, we welcome the 44th government onboarded to Have I Been Pwned’s free gov service: The Bahamas. The National Computer Incident Response Team of The Bahamas, CIRT-BS, now has access to monitor government domains against the data in HIBP. As the national CIRT, CIRT-BS is responsible for c

items.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
items.summary: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

items.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
items.summary: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

items.title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
items.summary: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.

items.title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
items.summary: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, threat actors heavily leveraged AI agents to support campaigns against entities in Mexico and Brazil.

items.title: Risky Business #837 -- GitHub Actions footgun claims TanStack
items.summary: On this week’s show Patrick Gray, Adam Boileau and James Wilson discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.summary: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- in

items.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
items.summary: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

items.summary: Today, we welcome the 42nd government onboarded to Have I Been Pwned’s free gov service: Costa Rica.The CSIRT of the Government of Costa Rica now has access to monitor government domains against the data in HIBP.
